content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a physical reflection of light and an internal cognitive act of反思, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms are missing the dual core of the concept: they only focus on internal error correction, not 'reflecting' inconsistent external perspectives back to the agent's original reasoning to trigger genuine reflection. This dual framing leads to a new self-reflection framework that combines physical-style reflection of external perspectives with internal cognitive reflection.
